answer:
We can test the options by checking if they satisfy all four statements.  

Option B: E true, others false.  
- (E ⊃ F) is false, so (E ⊃ F) ⊃ G is true (false ⊃ false).  
- (~E ∨ F) is false, (H ∨ I) is false, so the biconditional is true.  
- I ⊃ (J ⊃ ~H) is true (false ⊃ anything).  
- ~G ⊃ ~J is true (true ⊃ true).  
All true, so consistent.  

\boxed{B}
